questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $T$ be the $\Delta$ with vertices $(0,0),\left(0, c^2\right)$ and $\left(c, c^2\right)$ and let $R$ be the region between $y=c x$ and $y=x^2$ where $c\gt0$ then
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Area }(T)=\frac{c \cdot c^2}{2}=\frac{c^3}{2} \\ & \text { Area }(R)=\frac{c^3}{2}-\int_0^c x^2 d x \\ & =\frac{c^3}{2}-\frac{c^3}{3}=\frac{c^3}{6} \\ & \lim _{c \rightarrow 0^{+}} \frac{\operatorname{Area}(T)}{\operatorname{Area}(R)}=\lim _{R \rightarrow 0^{+}} \frac{c^3}{2} \cdot \frac{6}{c^3}=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $P(x)$ be a polynomial satisfying $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{x^2 P(x)}{x^5+5 x+6}=2$ and $P(1)=2, P(2)=16$, $P(3)=54$, then:
options: area bounded by $y=f(x), x=0, x=2$ and $x$-axis is 4 sq. units.
area bounded by $y=f(x), x=0, x=2$ and $x$-axis is 8 sq. units.
questions.solution:
Clearly, $P(x)$ is a polynomial of degree three with leading coefficient 2 .
Clearly, $\quad P(x)=2 x^3$
$\therefore \quad P(4)=128$ and area $=\int_0^2 2 x^3 d x=\left(\frac{x^4}{2}\right)_0^2=8$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
